name: Moosend API
what_it_does: Moosend REST API — affordable email marketing automation with subscriber management, campaign creation, and behavioral automation sequences, providing a Mailchimp-alternative with competitive pricing for SMBs and startups.
use_cases: Agents managing email subscriber lists on a budget — adding contacts and maintaining list hygiene via Moosend API
Automated campaign scheduling — agents creating and sending marketing campaigns based on content calendar triggers
Behavioral automation — agents triggering Moosend automation sequences based on user actions in external systems
E-commerce email — agents sending cart abandonment and purchase follow-up sequences via Moosend workflows
Subscriber segmentation — agents building audience segments based on behavior and engagement data for targeted sends
not_for: Large enterprise email marketing — Salesforce Marketing Cloud or HubSpot have better enterprise workflow capabilities
Transactional email at scale — use Resend, Postmark, or SendGrid for high-volume transactional sending
Advanced attribution and analytics — Klaviyo has much deeper behavioral and revenue attribution for e-commerce
best_when: You need affordable email marketing automation for an SMB budget with a clean REST API and decent automation features.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-grade automation or deep e-commerce integration — Klaviyo is far better for revenue-attributed email automation.

auth.notes: API key from Moosend account settings. Passed as query parameter or header. Account-wide access with no scope granularity. Key is revocable from settings.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 30-day trial with full features; no permanent free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: From $9/month for up to 500 subscribers; scales with subscriber count; unlimited emails on all paid plans
pricing.notes: Competitive pricing compared to Mailchimp — typically 50-70% cheaper for same subscriber count. No email sending limits on paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: No published hard rate limits; practical limits apply; bulk operations recommended for large lists

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is functional but not as polished as larger platforms. Error responses include codes and messages. Rate limits not clearly documented. API is v3 with reasonable stability.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Subscriber add is idempotent by email — existing subscribers are updated. Campaign creation is not idempotent. Campaign sends are irreversible.
known_agent_gotchas: API key passed as query parameter by default — ensure HTTPS to prevent key exposure in server logs
Mailing list IDs are GUIDs — agents must pre-fetch list IDs before subscriber operations
Campaign sends are irreversible — no undo once send is triggered; implement confirmation gate before sending
Automation triggers are configuration-based — agents cannot programmatically create new automation sequences
Rate limits undocumented — implement conservative request pacing (max 5 req/s) to avoid silent throttling

security_score_components.security_notes: GDPR compliant — EU-based company with EU data hosting. TLS enforced. API key with no scope granularity and query parameter exposure risk. No SOC2 documented. Smaller company — less mature security posture.
